pattern: Treating each clip as a jump from its start to its end turns minimal coverage into a greedy reach-extension: from the covered prefix, always grab the clip that pushes the frontier furthest.
hints: For every possible start second, record the farthest end reachable by any clip beginning at or before it.
This becomes the classic jump-game reach: track the current covered end and the best end reachable within it.
Sweep from 0; whenever you exhaust the current reach you must spend one more clip to extend it to the best reachable end. If that frontier ever fails to advance before covering time, return -1.

edge_inputs.description: overlapping clips cover with three picks
gap in the middle makes it impossible
many small clips, greedy picks the long jumps
single clip exactly covers the interval

stress.description: random clips with guaranteed full coverage so the answer is finite
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    T = rng.randint(5, 100)
    clips = []
    # ensure coverage: chain of unit clips
    for i in range(T):
        clips.append([i, i + 1])
    extra = max(0, min(size, 100) - T)
    for _ in range(extra):
        a = rng.randint(0, T)
        b = rng.randint(a, min(100, a + 30))
        clips.append([a, b])
    rng.shuffle(clips)
    return [clips, T]
stress.note: unit-clip chain guarantees coverage; answer is never -1
